articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Major Concerns:** 82% of landlords are worried about the implications of the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% expressing strong concerns.  
- **Portfolio Shrinkage:** 75% of landlords feel regulations deter portfolio growth, with 49% considering selling or reducing their investments in the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, with a looming 2030 mandate to upgrade rental properties to a C rating.  
- Rising energy costs from the Iran War have heightened tenants' interest in energy-efficient homes to save on expenses.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Only 24% of UK landlords believe that recent planning reforms will benefit their business.  
- A lack of confidence in planning changes signals potential challenges for the property market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in rental properties come from outside, highlighting the need for landlords to prioritize external pipework maintenance.  
- Leak detection expert Aspect cautions that neglecting outdoor plumbing can lead to costly repairs for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will introduce significant changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, focusing on balancing rights between landlords and tenants.  
- Landlords must prepare for new regulations and oversight under the Act, making it essential to stay informed and compliant ahead of the impending deadline.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Majority Unprepared**: A staggering 84% of landlords are not ready for the upcoming Renters' Rights Act, set to take effect on May 1.
- **High Compliance Risk**: 58% of landlords are at significant risk of failing to comply with the new regulations, indicating widespread uncertainty in the rental market.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting from expansion to restructuring portfolios to adapt to changing rental demand amid slowing rental growth, as highlighted by ONS data.  
- The focus is now on optimizing financing and portfolio strategies to align with the current market conditions.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are significantly driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- The surge in property purchases by landlords is escalating affordability issues for aspiring homeowners and residents.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the upcoming government to develop a dedicated strategy to support the vital role of the private rented sector (PRS).  
- The call highlights concerns over a diminishing landlord presence and emphasizes the need for formal recognition of the PRS's importance in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting May, HMRC will enforce mandatory registration for tax advisors, impacting conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams.
- Non-compliance could lead to hefty penalties, with fines reaching up to £10,000.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that new licensing fees for landlords, set at £694 per property, will not result in higher rental prices for tenants.
- Landlords express concern about absorbing costs without any financial recovery, potentially impacting property management practices.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook confirms the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The stance aims to address housing affordability concerns without imposing strict regulations on landlords.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council is taking action to regulate the housing market by drafting comprehensive guidelines for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs).
- The move aims to curb the uncontrolled growth of HMO landlords in the area, ensuring better oversight and standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long initiative kicks off Monday to educate tenants on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The campaign aims to empower renters with knowledge and tools to navigate their rights effectively.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act has led to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, confirming concerns voiced by property expert Paul Shamplina.  
- The legislative changes are straining tenant-landlord relationships, sparking debate on their long-term impact on the rental market.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are climbing as the Renters Rights Act approaches, highlighting market shifts ahead of anticipated legislative changes.
- New data from Hamptons indicates a strong rental demand that could further influence pricing dynamics.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act is poised to significantly impact the rental market, possibly driving away hobbyist landlords.  
- Industry experts anticipate that the new legislation, set to take effect in just 10 days, will alter the landscape of private renting in the UK.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly entering the property investment market, driven by a desire to build wealth despite regulatory challenges.
- Rising taxes and red tape haven’t deterred this generation; instead, they view real estate as a valuable avenue for financial growth.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- House Buyer Bureau highlights a challenging market for sellers, with many needing to lower prices, face failed sales, or exit the market altogether.  
- Chris Hodgkinson warns that ongoing difficulties are reshaping the selling landscape in the UK property market.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its review of property valuations, leading to a rise in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This increased scrutiny is contributing to higher Inheritance Tax receipts for the government.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A property listing in Penrith, Cumbria for £135k inadvertently featured a sleeping tenant captured in the photos and video.  
- The surprising camouflaged tenant highlights the quirky challenges of showcasing homes for sale.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force labels referral fees as an "unseen surcharge" that adds to the costs for homebuyers in the UK.  
- This practice is criticized as a "hidden tax," highlighting the need for greater transparency in property transactions.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is extending its reach into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group set to manage its portfolio in the West.
- David Alexander highlights this strategic expansion as a significant step for Lomond's growth in the UK property market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributed to unexpected declines in new buyer activity amid geopolitical tensions.  
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights that the current market challenges are significantly impacting real estate demand.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising inflation, fueled by Middle East conflict, is significantly affecting fuel and transport costs.  
- Experts warn that these price increases are likely to impact the broader UK economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of newly built homes never make it to the open sales market, reveals Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto.  
- This trend highlights significant barriers to market access for new properties in the UK.
